text.title: stakeholder-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for stakeholder-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent invents plausible-sounding stakeholders ("VP of Product Excellence") not present in the org chart.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hallucinated stakeholders produce engagement plans with no real recipients; always seed with a real org chart export and reject any name not in the source list unless flagged suggested_to_verify.

failure-mode.description: Agent over-classifies all stakeholders as "supportive" (politeness bias).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs are trained on polite discourse and default to positive sentiment; use a 4-way enum (+/0/-/unknown) with unknown as the default and require evidence for + or -.

failure-mode.description: Agent conflates stakeholder with persona ("As a Busy Manager persona...").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A stakeholder is a real named human or role with decision power; a persona is a synthesized archetype for design. Conflating them produces engagement plans targeting archetypes, not real people with authority.

failure-mode.description: Agent equates influence with seniority — ranks CFO highest influence by default.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Influence flows through indirect networks; the CFO's EA may have more influence on calendar decisions than the CFO. Force the agent to consider gatekeepers, information brokers, and informal leaders, not just org chart level.

failure-mode.description: Agent bulk-reclassifies 50 stakeholders in a single run.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bulk reclassification of attitude fields is reputationally toxic if wrong; cap agent actions to 5 stakeholders per run and require human review on the diff.
